instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
output: En el principio era la Palabra, y la Palabra estaba con Dios, y la Palabra era Dios.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He was in the beginning with God.
output: Aquel que es la Palabra estaba en el principio con Dios.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: All things came into being through Him, and apart from Him not one thing came into being which has come into being.
output: Todas las cosas por medio de Él llegaron a existir, y sin Él nada de cuanto existe ha llegado a la existencia.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: In Him was life, and the life was the light of men.
output: En Él estaba la vida, y la vida era la luz de los hombres.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And the light shines in the darkness, and the darkness did not overcome it.
output: La luz en las tinieblas resplandece, y las tinieblas no prevalecieron contra ella.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: There came a man sent from God, whose name was John.
output: Hubo un hombre enviado de Dios, el cual se llamaba Juan.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He came for a testimony that he might testify concerning the light, that all might believe through him.
output: Éste vino por testimonio, para que diese testimonio de la luz, a fin de que todos creyesen por él.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He was not the light, but came that he might testify concerning the light.
output: No era él la luz, pero vino para dar testimonio de la luz.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: This was the true light which, coming into the world, enlightens every man.
output: Aquél era la luz verdadera que, con Su venida al mundo, ilumina a todo hombre.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He was in the world, and the world came into being through Him, yet the world did not know Him.
output: En el mundo estaba, y por medio de Él llegó a existir el mundo; pero el mundo no le conoció.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He came to His own, yet those who were His own did not receive Him.
output: A lo Suyo vino, y los Suyos no le recibieron.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: But as many as received Him, to them He gave the authority to become children of God, to those who believe into His name,
output: Mas a todos los que le recibieron, a los que creen en Su nombre, les dio autoridad de ser hechos hijos de Dios;

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Who were begotten not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.
output: los cuales no son engendrados de sangre, ni de voluntad de carne, ni de voluntad de varón, sino de Dios.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And the Word became flesh and tabernacled among us (and we beheld His glory, glory as of the only Begotten from the Father), full of grace and reality.
output: Y la Palabra se hizo carne, y fijó tabernáculo entre nosotros (y contemplamos Su gloria, gloria como del Unigénito del Padre), llena de gracia y de realidad.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: John testified concerning Him and cried out, saying, This was He of whom I said, He who is coming after me has become ahead of me, because He was before me.
output: Juan dio testimonio de Él, y clamó diciendo: Éste es de quien yo decía: El que viene después de mí, se ha puesto delante de mí; porque era primero que yo.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: For of His fullness we have all received, and grace upon grace.
output: Porque de Su plenitud recibimos todos, y gracia sobre gracia.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: For the law was given through Moses; grace and reality came through Jesus Christ.
output: Pues la ley por medio de Moisés fue dada, pero la gracia y la realidad vinieron por medio de Jesucristo.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: No one has ever seen God; the only begotten Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, He has declared Him.
output: A Dios nadie le vio jamás; el unigénito Hijo, que está en el seno del Padre, Él le ha dado a conocer.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And this is the testimony of John, when the Jews sent to him priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, Who are you?
output: Éste es el testimonio de Juan, cuando los judíos enviaron de Jerusalén sacerdotes y levitas para que le preguntasen: ¿Tú, quién eres?

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And he confessed and did not deny, and he confessed, I am not the Christ.
output: Confesó, y no negó, sino confesó: Yo no soy el Cristo.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And they asked him, What then? Are you Elijah? And he said, I am not. Are you the Prophet? And he answered, No.
output: Y le preguntaron: ¿Qué pues? ¿Eres tú Elías? Dijo: No soy. ¿Eres tú el Profeta? Y respondió: No.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: They said then to him, Who are you, that we may give an answer to those who sent us? What do you say about yourself?
output: Entonces le dijeron: ¿Quién eres?, para que demos respuesta a los que nos enviaron. ¿Qué dices de ti mismo?

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He said, I am “a voice of one crying in the wilderness, Make straight the way of the Lord!” as Isaiah the prophet said.
output: Dijo: Yo soy la “voz de uno que clama en el desierto: ¡Enderezad el camino del Señor!”, como dijo el profeta Isaías.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And those who had been sent were of the Pharisees.
output: Y los que habían sido enviados eran de los fariseos.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And they asked him and said to him, Why then are you baptizing if you are not the Christ nor Elijah nor the Prophet?
output: Y le preguntaron, y le dijeron: ¿Por qué, pues, bautizas, si tú no eres el Cristo, ni Elías, ni el Profeta?

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: John answered them, saying, I baptize in water; but among you stands One whom you do not know,
output: Juan les respondió diciendo: Yo bautizo en agua; mas en medio de vosotros está uno a quien vosotros no conocéis.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He who is coming after me, the thong of whose sandal I am not worthy to untie.
output: Éste es el que viene después de mí, a quien yo no soy digno de desatar la correa de Sus sandalias.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: These things took place in Bethany across the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
output: Estas cosas sucedieron en Betania, al otro lado del Jordán, donde Juan estaba bautizando.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: The next day he saw Jesus coming to him and said, Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
output: El siguiente día vio Juan a Jesús que venía a él, y dijo: ¡He aquí el Cordero de Dios, que quita el pecado del mundo!

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: This is He of whom I said, A man is coming after me who has become ahead of me, because He was before me.
output: Éste es Aquel de quien yo dije: Después de mí viene un varón, el cual se ha puesto delante de mí; porque era primero que yo.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And I did not know Him, but in order that He might be manifested to Israel, for this reason I came baptizing in water.
output: Y yo no le conocía; mas para que fuese manifestado a Israel, por esto vine yo bautizando en agua.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And John testified, saying, I beheld the Spirit descending as a dove out of heaven, and He abode upon Him.
output: También dio Juan testimonio, diciendo: Vi al Espíritu que descendía del cielo como paloma, y permaneció sobre Él.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And I did not know Him, but He who sent me to baptize in water, He said to me, He upon whom you see the Spirit descending and abiding upon Him, this is He who baptizes in the Holy Spirit.
output: Y yo no le conocía; pero el que me envió a bautizar en agua, Él me dijo: Sobre quien veas descender al Espíritu y que permanece sobre Él, ése es el que bautiza en el Espíritu Santo.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And I have seen and have testified that this is the Son of God.
output: Y yo le vi, y he dado testimonio de que éste es el Hijo de Dios.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Again the next day John was standing with two of his disciples,
output: El siguiente día otra vez estaba Juan, y dos de sus discípulos.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And looking at Jesus walking, he said, Behold, the Lamb of God!
output: Y mirando a Jesús que andaba por allí, dijo: ¡He aquí el Cordero de Dios!

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And the two disciples heard him speak this, and they followed Jesus.
output: Le oyeron hablar los dos discípulos, y siguieron a Jesús.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And Jesus, turning and beholding them following, said to them, What are you seeking? And they said to Him, Rabbi (which translated means Teacher), where are You staying?
output: Y volviéndose Jesús, y viendo que le seguían, les dijo: ¿Qué buscáis? Ellos le dijeron: Rabí (que traducido es, Maestro), ¿dónde moras?

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He said to them, Come, and you will see. They went therefore and saw where He was staying, and they stayed with Him that day; it was about the tenth hour.
output: Les dijo: Venid y ved. Fueron, y vieron donde moraba, y se quedaron con Él aquel día; era como la hora décima.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: One of the two who heard this from John and followed Him was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
output: Andrés, hermano de Simón Pedro, era uno de los dos que habían oído lo que había dicho Juan, y habían seguido a Jesús.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah (which translated means Christ).
output: Él halló primero a su hermano Simón, y le dijo: Hemos hallado al Mesías (que traducido es, el Cristo).

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He led him to Jesus. Looking at him, Jesus said, You are Simon, the son of John; you shall be called Cephas (which is interpreted, Peter).
output: Y le trajo a Jesús. Y mirándole Jesús, dijo: Tú eres Simón, hijo de Jonás; tú serás llamado Cefas (que quiere decir, Pedro).

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: The next day He wanted to go forth into Galilee, and He found Philip. And Jesus said to him, Follow Me.
output: El siguiente día quiso Jesús ir a Galilea, y halló a Felipe, y le dijo: Sígueme.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.
output: Y Felipe era de Betsaida, la ciudad de Andrés y Pedro.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found Him of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, wrote, Jesus, the son of Joseph, from Nazareth.
output: Felipe halló a Natanael, y le dijo: Hemos hallado a Aquel de quien escribió Moisés en la ley, así como los profetas: a Jesús, el hijo de José, de Nazaret.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And Nathanael said to him, Can anything good be from Nazareth? Philip said to him, Come and see.
output: Natanael le dijo: ¿De Nazaret puede salir algo bueno? Le dijo Felipe: Ven y ve.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to Him and said concerning him, Behold, truly an Israelite, in whom there is no guile!
output: Cuando Jesús vio a Natanael que se le acercaba, dijo de él: ¡He aquí un verdadero israelita, en quien no hay engaño!

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Nathanael said to Him, How do You know me? Jesus answered and said to him, Before Philip called you, while you were under the fig tree, I saw you.
output: Le dijo Natanael: ¿Cómo es que me conoces? Respondió Jesús y le dijo: Antes que Felipe te llamara, cuando estabas debajo de la higuera, te vi.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Nathanael answered Him, Rabbi, You are the Son of God; You are the King of Israel.
output: Respondió Natanael y le dijo: Rabí, Tú eres el Hijo de Dios; Tú eres el Rey de Israel.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Jesus answered and said to him, Is it because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree that you believe? You shall see greater things than these.
output: Respondió Jesús y le dijo: ¿Porque te dije: Te vi debajo de la higuera, crees? Cosas mayores que éstas verás.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And He said to him, Truly, truly, I say to you, You shall see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.
output: Y le dijo: De cierto, de cierto os digo: Veréis el cielo abierto, y a los ángeles de Dios subir y descender sobre el Hijo del Hombre.
